_templateNotes: Fill this file locally after running the Today real-feed pilot.
Use the canonical camelCase keys shown here. The parser still accepts older alias keys, but new local evidence should stay in this normalized format.
Keep local/private evidence files out of git.
_mergeGuidance: Keep updating one local/private evidence file across multiple pilot passes.
If you capture a real_empty run and a separate real-card run, merge both observations into the same local/private evidence file instead of committing multiple files.
Use emptyStateChecks, enrichedSummaryCases, deterministicFallbackCases, freshnessNotes, and sourceCoverageNotes to record evidence gathered across separate sessions.

realEmptyDistinctFromFilterEmpty: Only mark this true after you intentionally observe a real_empty state that is clearly different from a normal filter-empty state.
Capture one screenshot or note describing why it was a genuine real_empty result and not an invalid env or generic fallback case.
Recommended updater flags: --observed-feed-mode real_empty --real-empty-distinct true --empty-state-check "Describe the genuine real_empty observation here."
completedNonEmptyEnrichedContentObserved: Only mark these true after you observe a real card where completed and non-empty enriched text is visibly present and clearly beats the deterministic preview text.
Capture which card you used and what the enriched text improved.
Recommended updater flags: --completed-enriched-text-observed true --completed-enriched-text-wins true --enriched-case "Describe the winning enriched-content example here."
completedBlankEnrichedContentFallbackWor: Only mark this true after you observe a completed-but-blank enrichment case that safely falls back to deterministic preview text.
Capture which card you used and what deterministic text remained visible.
Recommended updater flags: --blank-enrichment-fallback true --deterministic-fallback-case "Describe the blank-enrichment fallback example here."
mobileQualityAcceptable: Check Today and Detail on at least one narrow/mobile viewport and record whether cards, chips, and provenance stayed readable.
Recommended updater flags: --mobile-quality acceptable --mobile-note "Describe the tested viewport and result here."
dataFreshnessAcceptable: Record whether the real cards looked fresh enough for this pilot, such as recent publish dates or clearly acceptable recency for the tested sources.
Recommended updater flags: --freshness acceptable --freshness-note "Describe the publish-date freshness evidence here."
sourceCoverageAcceptable: Record whether the observed real cards came from a broad enough mix of stable sources to justify future rollout discussion.
Recommended updater flags: --source-coverage acceptable --source-count 2 --source-coverage-note "Describe the observed source mix here."
